heading: Restoration or replacement of damaged or destroyed facilities
label: 2854 Restoration or replacement of damaged or destroyed facilities

children.label: a
children.text: Subject to subsection (b), the Secretary concerned may repair, restore, or replace a facility under his jurisdiction, including a family housing facility, that has been damaged or destroyed.

children.label: b
children.text: When a decision is made to carry out construction under subsection (a) and the cost of the repair, restoration, or replacement is greater than the maximum amount for a minor construction project, the Secretary concerned shall notify the appropriate committees of Congress of that decision, of the justification for the project, of the current estimate of the cost of the project, of the source of funds for the project, and of the justification for carrying out the project under this section. The project may then be carried out only after the end of the 14-day period beginning on the date the notification is received by such committees in an electronic medium pursuant to .section 480 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the damage or destruction to the facility was the result of a natural disaster or a terrorism incident; and

children.label: i
children.text: the current estimate of the cost of the replacement project;

children.label: ii
children.text: the source of funds for the replacement project;

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of damage to a facility rather than destruction, a certification that the replacement project is more cost-effective than repair or restoration; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a certification that deferral of the replacement project for inclusion in the next Military Construction Authorization Act would be inconsistent with national security or the protection of health, safety, or environmental quality, as the case may be.

A replacement project under this subsection may be carried out only after the end of the 7-day period beginning on the date on which a copy of the notification described in paragraph (1) is provided in an electronic medium pursuant to .section 480 of this title

The maximum aggregate amount that the Secretary concerned may obligate from appropriations available for operation and maintenance in any fiscal year for replacement projects under the authority of this subsection is $300,000,000.
